channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The DNC's outgoing chair says Democrats should have stuck with Joe Biden in 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the Democratic National Committee prepares to elect a new chair, its departing leader says Democrats should have stuck with Joe Biden in the 2024 race.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mushers and dogs braved a horrific Alaska winter to deliver lifesaving serum 100 years ago
item.description: The Alaska Gold Rush town of Nome was hundreds of miles from anywhere, cut off by the frozen sea and under siege from a contagious disease known as the “strangling angel” for the way it suffocated children.
item.dc:creator: Mark Thiessen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Midair collision kills 67 people in the deadliest US air disaster in almost a quarter century
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — A midair collision between an Army helicopter and a jetliner killed all 67 people aboard the two aircraft, officials said Thursday, as they scrutinized the actions of the military pilot in the country's deadliest aviation disaster in almost a quarter century.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, TARA COPP, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and LEA SKENE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Student, attorney among Ohioans killed in D.C. plane crash
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) -- A 20-year-old student at an Ohio university was among those killed Wednesday night when an American Airlines passenger jet collided with an Army helicopter in Washington D.C. Grace Maxwell was a junior at Cedarville University.
item.dc:creator: Brian Hofmann, NewsNation

item.title: Judge extends court-monitoring agreement for children in Customs and Border Protection custody
item.description: A federal judge filed a last-minute order extending a court agreement ensuring protections for children in federal custody the day after it ended.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Gonzalez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Big Oil wants a lot from Trump. It has an ally in Doug Burgum, the president's Interior pick
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — When North Dakota's petroleum association was going to hold a banquet honoring top fracking executives last year, it turned to Gov. Doug Burgum.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN SLODYSKO and JACK DURA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Conspiracies, espionage, an enemies list: Takeaways from a wild day of confirmation hearings
item.description: President Donald Trump’s most controversial Cabinet nominees have flooded the zone in back-to-back-to-back confirmation hearings.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Honolulu police have arrested 6 people in connection with deadly New Year’s fireworks blast
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Police arrested a total of six people in connection with a deadly explosion of illegal fireworks in a Honolulu neighborhood on New Year’s Eve and confiscated 500 pounds (227 kilograms) of unused fireworks from the scene of the blast, authorities said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Here's what is known about the deadly collision between a passenger jet and Army helicopter
item.description: A jet with 60 passengers and four crew members collided with an Army helicopter while approaching Ronald Reagan National Airport near Washington, D.C., sending the two aircraft plummeting into the Potomac River and killing everyone on board. The collision occurred at around 9 p.m.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN and DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Republicans leave Florida frustrated with lack of final plan to pass Trump agenda
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) arrived at this week’s House Republican retreat with hopes of uniting the fractious GOP conference around a plan to pass President Trump's agenda — but instead, the group is departing south Florida with rising tensions in its ranks. Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: White House aide says spending freeze isn't being rescinded despite memo
item.description: The White House is claiming the Wednesday move by the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) rescinding a controversial order that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance is not actually an end to curbing government spending. “This is NOT a rescission of the federal funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans advance Trump OMB nominee as Democrats boycott vote
item.description: Senate Republicans advanced the nomination of Russell Vought to lead the White House budget office as Democrats boycotted the meeting to protest the administration's recent efforts to freeze large swaths of federal funding.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard battles senators over Snowden, Syria in fiery hearing: 5 takeaways
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard, President Trump’s pick to be the next director of national intelligence, endured a difficult hearing before the Senate Intelligence Committee on Thursday as lawmakers pressed her on her views on Edward Snowden, spying, Syria and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: GOP senator 'surprised' Gabbard refused to call Snowden a traitor
item.description: Sen. James Lankford (R-Okla.), a member of the Senate Intelligence Committee, said he was “surprised” that director of national intelligence nominee Tulsi Gabbard refused to call former NSA contractor Edward Snowden a “traitor” after being repeatedly pressed on the question at a Thursday hearing.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Musk mocks Nazi salute accusations with puns
item.description: Elon Musk referenced Adolf Hitler and other Nazi leaders in a social media post filled with puns early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KTLA 5

item.title: LISTEN: Audio captures air traffic control at moment of DC crash
item.description: Audio of air traffic control captured the moment that workers observed a Black Hawk helicopter colliding with a passenger jet near Reagan National Airport on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, Fox 5

item.title: South Carolina death row inmate maintains his innocence and won't ask for clemency
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — After the U.S. Supreme Court rejected the final appeal of a South Carolina inmate, he decided not to ask the governor for clemency, saying he didn't want to spend the rest of his life in prison for a murder he has always insisted he did not commit. Marion Bowman Jr.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Black Caucus blasts Trump's DEI comments after Potomac plane crash
item.description: "Not only are the President’s claims untrue, they also speak to the Republican Party’s desire to divide us as a country.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KRON4

item.title: A police officer testifies that an ex-prosecutor never obstructed her in the Ahmaud Arbery case
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — The case against a former Georgia district attorney charged with discouraging police from arresting the man who killed Ahmaud Arbery in 2020 took a blow Thursday as a police supervisor testified that the accused prosecutor never obstructed her during the investigation.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NTSB leads investigation into deadly DC plane crash
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board is leading an investigation into how the American Airlines plane collided with the military helicopter.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Brandt, KRON4

item.title: Staffing at Reagan Washington National Airport air control tower was 'not normal' on night of collision: FAA
item.description: The staffing at the air control tower at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport was "not normal" when an American Airlines plane and an Army helicopter collided on Wednesday night, a report shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aviation expert weighs in on how aircraft crash near DC may have happened
item.description: An aviation expert told Nexstar's WDCW that the altitude of both the passenger plane and Army helicopter may have played a principal role in the aircraft collision near Reagan National Airport (DCA).
item.dc:creator: Anna Chen, KRON4

item.title: Dwyane Wade reveals cancer diagnosis, kidney surgery
item.description: The Chicago native shared the story of the "shocking" diagnosis and the emotional toll it took on him on the latest episode of his 'The Why' podcast.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, NewsNation

item.title: Reagan National Airport one of world's most complex airspaces
item.description: Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport (DCA) services both military helicopters and passenger planes every day.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: FCC chair launches investigation into NPR, PBS
item.description: The chairman of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) is launching an investigation into NPR and PBS over their alleged "airing of commercials.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI wrestles with a spike in sexual misconduct claims and male-dominated culture
item.description: An investigation by AP revealed that the incidents ranged from sexual assault to agents paying for sex at massage parlors.
item.dc:creator: Jim Mustian, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: One of the Oklahoma's best high school wrestling team's season suspended
item.description: A disciplinary decision from the Oklahoma Secondary Schools Activities Association (OSSAA) has ended the season for the girls wrestling team at Edmond North High School.
item.dc:creator: Sydnee Batzlaff/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Man recovering after allegedly being stabbed by girlfriend's father
item.description: 24-year-old Jonathan Washington was walking home from his girlfriend's house when he was hit by a truck and nearly stabbed to death.
item.dc:creator: Mecca Thompson/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles fire cleanup complicated by 'unprecedented' number of EVs with combustible lithium-ion batteries
item.description: California wildfire cleanup is complicated by highly combustible lithium-ion batteries found in electric and hybrid vehicles popular in Los Angeles County.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missouri woman pardoned for Jan. 6 offenses sentenced in deadly drunken-driving crash
item.description: Emily Hernandez, a Missouri woman pardoned after her ties to the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the U.S. Capitol, was sentenced Wednesday in connection with a drunken-driving crash.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate Democrats revolt against Trump in fallout from foreign aid freeze
item.description: Senate Democrats are starting to revolt against President Trump’s agenda on Capitol Hill amid fallout from an initial freeze on all foreign assistance, warning Republicans that failure to work together will incur consequences. Sen. Chris Coons (D-Del.) on Thursday reversed support for Rep.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Video captures fiery crash between American Airlines flight and Army Blackhawk
item.description: Editor's note: The video may appear graphic to some viewers. (NEXSTAR) - Images from the EarthCam video service captured images of what appears to be the fiery crash of an American Airlines jet over the Potomac River Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hegseth: Army battalion involved in DCA crash grounded for 48 hours
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said he has grounded the Army battalion involved in the operation of the Black Hawk helicopter with three soldiers that crashed into a passenger plane with more than 60 people on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Figure skaters among those on flight that collided with Army helicopter
item.description: U.S. Figure Skating released a statement saying athletes, coaches, and family members were on their way home from the National Development Camp held in conjunction with the U.S. Figure Skating Championship in Kansas when the collision happened.
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, WGN 9

item.title: Elite university attended by Trump’s son cracks down on left-wing student agitators
item.description: Almost a dozen students have been suspended for a year from NYU for previous protests as President Trump's no-nonsense approach to campus protests takes hold.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Collision between passenger jet and Army helicopter near DC leaves no survivors
item.description: There were no survivors after a collision between an American Airlines passenger jet and an Army helicopter at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport near Washington, D.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2
